title: primula × helenae
intro: Primula × helenae, also known as Helen's primrose, is a hybrid plant species derived from the genus Primula, characterized by its unique combination of primrose-like and auricula-like features. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its ability to soothe the stomach and intestines. The health benefits of Primula × helenae include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. In herbal preparations, Primula × helenae is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
